context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=13; meal=bite-sized pizza and broccoli; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit or other cold-served food AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose no reheating for a fruit breakfast. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a longer reheat time for hot savory meals in that social-right context, including a sauce-accompanied savory meal. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose a longer reheat time for savory meals while working, including a front-laptop evening meal. IF food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies or sauce-relevant items AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for that TV meal, indicating preference for serving non-fruit breakfast items warm. IF food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the longest reheating time in that left-TV savory lunch, indicating a stronger hot-food preference in that context. IF food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar handheld savory item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right, THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user chose only light reheating in that right-TV evening meal, suggesting a milder warming preference for that food-context combination. IF food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the new front-TV dinner shows a stronger preference for hotter savory food in that context than earlier front-TV meals.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized preserving visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with sauce or dip, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently prioritized keeping the right side visually open for social engagement. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the front visual field while using a front-facing device. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed meal including dessert or sauce-relevant items, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user protected the right visual field when the attended device was on the right. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization while attending to a front-facing TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly protected the left visual field when the attention target was on the left. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly did not request left-side visibility protection in that left-laptop context, suggesting occlusion sensitivity tracks the salience of the attended target rather than simply its direction.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly chose faster robot motion in that simple meal context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed or more complex meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for a more complex front-TV meal. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN slow BECAUSE the new front-TV dinner shows a stronger comfort/caution preference for a hot savory meal in that context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user consistently chose medium speed in repeated right-social savory meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory or mixed meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user consistently chose medium speed in repeated laptop-engaged meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar savory handheld meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in that right-TV meal, reinforcing medium as the user's typical balance of comfort and efficiency. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slower motion in that left-TV lunch, indicating greater caution or comfort-seeking for a hot, sauce-involving meal in that context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is typical skewered solid foods such as meats, nuggets, broccoli-containing savory meals, potato pieces, bite-sized sandwiches, or bite-sized pizza pieces, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option across multiple savory meals. IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is soft dessert-like or mixed sweet items such as brownies, including mixed meals with brownies and sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-acquisition-success option for brownie-containing meals, suggesting willingness to trade bite narrowness for acquisition reliability for soft foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid or savory meal, including sauce-accompanied savory meals, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose confirmation in that social context, consistent with preferring extra verification when failed transfers would be more awkward. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid, savory, or mixed dessert-containing meal, including sauce-accompanied meals, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose explicit bite-acquisition confirmation while working, indicating a stable preference for verification in attention-divided contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that simpler meal, consistent with preferring efficiency when acquisition is likely straightforward. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed, sauce-involving, or hot savory meal, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for more complex front-TV meals, indicating that food complexity in that context triggers a preference for verification. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose confirmation in a more complex TV meal, reinforcing that complexity and divided attention favor explicit verification. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to skip confirmation there, suggesting that in right-TV contexts the user may favor efficiency when the meal seems straightforward and low-mess.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or right-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any previously observed food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and has usually selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user explicitly switched to direct delivery in that context, indicating that when attention is directed to a side TV during a more complex meal the user may prefer reduced self-motion and more robot assistance. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the new front-TV dinner shows that for some hotter or more effortful savory meals the user prefers more direct delivery even in a front-facing media context.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth stopping distance does not apply when the robot delivers directly into the mouth. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a routine meal AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance in the routine setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose a slightly larger buffer in that social setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user repeatedly returned to the closer stopping distance in laptop-engaged contexts. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the closer stopping distance when outside-mouth transfer was selected. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN near BECAUSE the user again selected the close stopping distance in that right-TV meal.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken readiness cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly switched to a quieter visual cue in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual cue while attending to TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal cue in that divided-attention context, indicating preference for a more noticeable signal when attention is directed leftward. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN speech BECAUSE the user chose spoken readiness cues in that right-TV meal, showing that right-TV contexts do not uniformly require visual-only signaling.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory front-TV meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can easily press buttons, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less suitable; the user has consistently selected explicit manual signaling across those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly shifted to lower-effort automation in that TV context, suggesting that when attention is occupied by side viewing the user may prefer the robot to proceed automatically rather than require manual input. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again chose automatic progression in a side-TV context, strengthening the pattern that side-TV attention can outweigh the user's usual preference for explicit button control.

IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory front-TV meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for drinking across those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic progression in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational preference for reduced interaction effort. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic progression in a side-TV context, reinforcing that side-oriented TV viewing can shift drinking initiation toward lower-effort automation.

IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory front-TV meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for mouth wiping across those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic progression in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational preference for reduced interaction effort. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic progression in a side-TV context, reinforcing that side-oriented TV viewing can shift wiping initiation toward lower-effort automation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken completion cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly switched to a visual completion cue in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual completion cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual completion cue while watching TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal completion cue in that divided-attention context, indicating preference for a more salient signal when attention is directed away from the robot. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN speech BECAUSE the user chose spoken completion cues in that right-TV meal, showing that side-TV contexts can still support speech-only signaling when the user prefers it.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory front-TV meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable automatic completion method for bites across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce in the right-social context, mixed dessert-containing meals in laptop-right work, and a savory front-TV meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion across those contexts. IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic completion in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational shift toward lower-effort automation for non-feeding tasks. IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic completion in a side-TV context, reinforcing that side-TV attention can shift drinking completion toward automation.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce in the right-social context, mixed dessert-containing meals in laptop-right work, and a savory front-TV meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion across those contexts. IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic completion in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational shift toward lower-effort automation for non-feeding tasks. IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic completion in a side-TV context, reinforcing that side-TV attention can shift wiping completion toward automation.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user previously chose to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in a right-side visibility context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user consistently chose retraction when right-side social engagement mattered. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted longer meal time to reduce obstruction while maintaining visibility of a front-facing task. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction when front visibility mattered. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user also chose retraction when a left-side attention target was present, reinforcing that salient visual engagement in any direction can motivate retraction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly did not request retraction in that left-laptop context, suggesting retraction depends on how strongly the user wants to preserve a particular visual channel rather than on leftward attention alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed meal including dessert or sauce-relevant items, THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to retract even while protecting right-side visibility, indicating that in some work contexts the user prioritizes uninterrupted efficiency over repeated clearing of the view. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user selected no retraction in that right-TV meal, indicating that right-side visibility concerns can be overridden when the user prefers a faster, less interrupted flow for a simpler meal.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is a meal without sauces or dips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping when dipping was unnecessary, including a front-TV savory meal without active dipping needs. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected restrained dipping in a right-social savory meal with ranch, consistent with a mess-averse compromise in social settings. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a simple fruit meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping despite sauce being available, consistent with an efficiency or low-mess preference in that meal. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose some dipping but limited the amount, suggesting a mess-averse compromise while multitasking. IF food_condition includes a savory meal where sauce is part of the dish rather than a separate dip to apply AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop in front, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user distinguished between optional dipping and already-sauced foods. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a mixed meal including dessert or sauce-relevant items, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected limited dipping rather than none, indicating a preference for modest flavor addition without heavy mess. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left with a savory meal, THEN less BECAUSE the user again chose limited dipping, reinforcing a stable preference for restrained sauce use in attention-divided contexts. IF food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar meal without active dipping needs AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in that right-TV meal.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a short timeout, consistent with efficient progression when explicit button controls are available. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose a very long timeout in that social setting, indicating a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when attention may be shared. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose a very long timeout while working, indicating preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short timeout in that simpler TV context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more complex mixed or sauce-involving meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long timeout there, indicating that in TV contexts food complexity can shift the user toward stronger protection against unwanted automatic continuation. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the new front-TV dinner shows an intermediate timeout preference, suggesting the user may want some protection against unwanted continuation without the extreme delay used for more complex or multitasking contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short timeout while also choosing several autocontinue-based controls, indicating a context-specific preference for efficient automatic flow rather than long interruption windows. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or similar simple savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user again chose a short timeout alongside multiple autocontinue settings, reinforcing that side-TV contexts can favor quick automatic progression.
